name: Auckland
summary: The home port of the two-masted schooner Emma, which cleared for Callao on February 20th with eleven men; Johansen returned here with yellow hair turned white before selling his cottage and sailing to Oslo.
description:
Auckland was the home port of the two-masted schooner Emma, which sailed for Callao on February 20th with a complement of eleven men. The Emma cleared in ballast, and felt the full force of the earthquake-born tempest which must have heaved up from the sea-bottom the horrors that filled men's dreams.

The Auckland correspondent of the Sydney Bulletin gave the Emma and her crew an excellent reputation, and described Johansen as a sober and worthy man.

It was to Auckland that Johansen returned, his yellow hair turned white, after a perfunctory and inconclusive questioning at Sydney; there he sold his cottage in West Street and sailed with his wife to his old home in Oslo — which is what the narrator learned when his own search brought him to the city.

sources.quote: had been second mate of the two-masted schooner Emma of Auckland, which sailed for Callao February 20th, with a complement of eleven men
sources.context: Chapter 3, The Madness from the Sea

sources.quote: Our Auckland correspondent gives the Emma and her crew an excellent reputation, and Johansen is described as a sober and worthy man.
